title: Distributed Governance Research: A Functional Architecture for Selective Institutional Distribution (Working Paper v1.2)
description: Complete research corpus and working paper proposing a functional architecture for distributed public project governance: citizens allocate a legally mandated share of an existing public budget across verifiable projects under strict role separation, milestone-gated conditional disbursement, evidential contracts, and role-based reputation. Includes formal mechanism-design propositions (incentive compatibility of milestone disbursement; collusion-proofness of protocolized fiscalization), a reproducible 10,000-agent simulation of attention-constrained allocation, and a documented adversarial validation record of thirty-five attack briefs with paired defenses and accepted resolutions, all propagated through the architecture corpus. The manuscript is drafts/paper.md; the accessible companion is docs/101_CORE_V0_MINIMAL_PUBLISHABLE_MODEL.md.
notes: Dual-licensed repository: research corpus CC BY 4.0; manuscript and reviewer packets CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 pending publication venue; code MIT. See LICENSE.md.
